title: Java Functional Interfaces
description: Practice Function, Predicate, and Consumer declarations.
learningGoals: Practice lambda assignments to functional interfaces.

choices: false, because "hi" has length 2, which is not greater than 8
true, because any non-empty string is long
Compilation fails because Predicate has no test method
completionChecks.explanation: isLong applies length (2) and checks > 8, which is false; Predicate<String>.test returns a boolean.

completionChecks.prompt: Which functional interface in the snippet transforms a String into an Integer?
completionChecks.explanation: Function<T, R> accepts a T and returns an R, so String::length fits Function<String, Integer>; Predicate returns boolean and Consumer returns nothing.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens when printer.accept("  ok  ") is called?
choices: Compilation fails because Consumer must return a value
completionChecks.explanation: Consumer.accept performs the lambda side effect and returns void; this lambda trims the text before printing it.
